Title: Polycomb repressive complex 2 facilitates the transition from heterotrophy to photoautotrophy during seedling emergence

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Storage lipid-derived carbon mobilization / glyoxylate shunt
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supports reserve lipid catabolism and carbon remobilization during germination; competes with establishment of photoautotrophic vegetative state when ectopically maintained
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Enables heterotrophic growth on storage reserves during germination; persistent activity delays transition to photoautotrophy
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling shoot/cotyledon-like tissues during early establishment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Peaks around 3 DAG and is repressed by 7 DAG during normal seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in clf swn shoots under both photoautotrophic and mixotrophic conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling emergence; 3 to 7 DAG transition; exogenous sucrose; PRC2 depletion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic ICL expression prevents or delays vegetative photoautotrophic transition and promotes embryonic reversal/TAG-accumulating phenotype; ICL knockout in clf swn reduces this phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICL gains high H3K27me3 between 3 and 7 DAG and is transcriptionally repressed during normal seedling establishment. 2. ICL is strongly derepressed in PRC2-depleted clf swn seedlings under both photoautotrophic and mixotrophic conditions. 3. CRISPR knockout of ICL in clf swn significantly reduces penetrance of the fat-red/TAG-accumulating phenotype and increases transition to the vegetative phase, demonstrating causal involvement.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for the glyoxylate-cycle contribution to seed-to-seedling metabolic state
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: H3K27me3 profiling; RT-qPCR; mutant transcriptomics; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout in clf swn; Sudan Red 7B TAG phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Repressing or editing ICL could help accelerate vegetative establishment and suppress prolonged heterotrophic/embryonic programs when manipulating epigenetic regulators; should be balanced against its normal role in reserve mobilization during germination
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reducing ICL may impair normal reserve lipid utilization during early germination under carbon-limited conditions
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Direct PRC2-mediated repression via increased H3K27me3 deposition between 3 and 7 DAG; derepressed in clf swn
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single-copy gene emphasized by authors as the key glyoxylate cycle gene tested functionally.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Reserve lipid-derived carbon conversion to sugars
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies sugar synthesis from reserve-derived carbon during postgerminative heterotrophy
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports conversion of storage reserves into carbohydrates during early seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High around 3 DAG and repressed by 7 DAG
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling emergence from 3 to 7 DAG; PRC2 depletion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Persistent expression is associated with prolonged early seedling heterotrophic metabolic program
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PCK1 gains H3K27me3 and is transcriptionally repressed between 3 and 7 DAG in WT shoot. 2. PCK1 is among metabolic genes ectopically activated in PRC2-depleted seedlings. 3. Its repression forms part of the second PRC2 phase that shuts down germination-associated metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key regulated gluconeogenic step during transition, but not directly functionally dissected
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced PCK1 could compromise early reserve mobilization
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Gains H3K27me3 between 3 and 7 DAG; PRC2-dependent repression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly edited in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Chromatin modifier; PRC2 histone methyltransferase subunit
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of seed maturation, storage lipid accumulation, glyoxylate cycle, gluconeogenesis, and broader seedling metabolic state transitions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICL; PCK1; LEC1; LEC2; FUS3; ABI3; storage reserve biosynthesis genes; other germination-associated metabolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses embryo maturation network before/by 3 DAG and represses germination/early seedling metabolic genes between 3 and 7 DAG
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental seed-to-seedling transition; photosynthesis status influences later-phase H3K27me3 dynamics indirectly
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PRC2 with SWN and other subunits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Storage lipids / TAG and reserve-mobilization metabolic state
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Proper repression prevents sucrose-induced embryonic storage-lipid accumulation and promotes photoautotrophic seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot/cotyledon-like structures in PRC2-depleted seedlings under sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied by loss-of-function mutant phenotype rather than expression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous sucrose; photoautotrophic vs mixotrophic growth; seedling emergence stages
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CLF together with SWN causes derepression of embryonic and germination metabolic programs, ectopic TAG accumulation, failure or delay of photoautotrophic transition, and reduced CO2 assimilation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CLF-containing PRC2 is required for two phases of repression during seedling establishment: embryo maturation genes before/by 3 DAG and germination-associated metabolic genes between 3 and 7 DAG. 2. PRC2 depletion causes sucrose-dependent reactivation of LAFL genes and TAG accumulation, plus sucrose-independent derepression of ICL/PCK1 and related metabolism. 3. These defects impair normal transition from heterotrophy to photoautotrophy.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major chromatin-level regulator enabling vegetative seedling establishment and suppressing embryonic storage metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mutants clf swn; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq/ChIP-qPCR; metabolomics; 13CO2 assimilation; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Epigenetic manipulation of PRC2-linked programs could tune seedling establishment and reserve-use transitions, but broad pleiotropy makes direct breeding deployment difficult
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe developmental defects, delayed germination, reduced growth, floral/homeotic abnormalities when PRC2 is depleted
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: CLF is itself a core H3K27 methyltransferase catalyzing repressive chromatin deposition
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core gene studied genetically as part of clf swn double mutant; direct causal regulator of nutrient-metabolism state transitions.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Chromatin modifier; PRC2 histone methyltransferase subunit
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PRC2-dependent repression of embryo maturation, storage lipid accumulation, glyoxylate cycle, gluconeogenesis, and seedling transition metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICL; PCK1; LEC1; LEC2; FUS3; ABI3; storage reserve biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with CLF to repress seed and early germination metabolic programs
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: PRC2 with CLF and other subunits
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Storage lipids / TAG and reserve-mobilization metabolic state
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppression of inappropriate storage-lipid accumulation and promotion of photoautotrophic growth
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Shoot/cotyledon-like structures in PRC2-depleted seedlings under sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Studied by mutant combination with clf
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous sucrose; photoautotrophic vs mixotrophic growth; seedling emergence stages
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss with CLF causes ectopic TAG accumulation and prolonged heterotrophic/embryonic metabolism, impairing normal seedling establishment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWN functions redundantly with CLF in PRC2 to repress two temporally distinct gene sets during seedling establishment. 2. Loss of SWN together with CLF deregulates LAFL genes and metabolic genes such as ICL and PCK1. 3. This drives embryonic reversal and defective photoautotrophic transition.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential co-regulator within PRC2 for suppressing seed storage metabolism after germination
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic mutants clf swn; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq/ChIP-qPCR; metabolomics; phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Only suitable as a mechanistic epigenetic engineering reference due to broad pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe developmental pleiotropy and impaired growth when depleted
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function interpreted together with CLF in the severe cs double mutant.
